name: did:aip with fully revoked key
description: The signing key has been revoked (on-chain account closed / key removed from DID Document entirely). Unlike rotation where the prior key remains in the history chain, revocation means the key is no longer valid for any purpose.
reason: A revoked key is not a rotated key. Revocation = key removed from all resolution paths. Receipts signed with revoked keys MUST be rejected.
validation_level: requires DID resolution (not detectable by format-only validator)

key_state.revocation_status: on-chain account closed, DID no longer resolves

name: did:web with key removed from DID Document
description: The signing key was not just rotated -- it was completely removed from the DID Document's verificationMethod array. The key no longer exists in any resolution path.
reason: Key not present in DID Document at all. Unlike kr-valid-002 where the key was demoted but present, here the key is gone. Receipt signed with a non-existent key MUST be rejected.
validation_level: requires DID resolution (not detectable by format-only validator)
